title: Anti-SB13 Campaigners Aren't Ignorant. They're Trying to Deceive You.
author: James Silberman
orientation: James Silberman, a former pro-life movement insider, argues that the anonymous website abolitiontruth.org is not honestly mistaken about Oklahoma's SB13 (the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act), but is deliberately deceiving the public, and he systematically refutes eight specific lies the site spreads about the bill, abolitionism, nullification, and the power of state government to end abortion.
audiences: Pro-life Oklahoman trying to evaluate SB13 honestly
Christian wrestling with judicial supremacy and Romans 13
State legislator considering an abolition bill
Abolitionist activist needing rebuttals to common attacks
Historian or civics-minded reader skeptical of nullification

questions.question: Does SB13 actually contain a severability clause that could let courts strike the bill while leaving abortion regulations repealed?
questions.audience: Pro-life Oklahoman trying to evaluate SB13 honestly
questions.answer: No. SB13 explicitly contains an inseverability clause, and Silberman argues the lobbyists claiming otherwise know the text of the bill and are lying intentionally. Even if a court did try to misapply the bill, that would only strengthen the case for SB13's stripping of judicial authority over the question.
questions.quote: In reality, Section 34 of SB13 reads: “The provisions, words, phrases, and clauses of this act are declared to be inseverable.” Your average pro-life person could be forgiven for not knowing that SB13 had an inseverability clause or for not knowing what an inseverability clause is. But the people making this claim are politicians and lobbyists. They know what an inseverability clause is and they know that SB13 has one.

questions.question: Is it true that SB13 “wouldn’t save a single life” because courts would immediately enjoin it?
questions.audience: Pro-life Oklahoman trying to evaluate SB13 honestly
questions.answer: Only if Oklahoma's executive branch capitulates to the courts. SB13 instructs the state to treat any contrary federal court decision as void, so whether the bill saves lives depends on whether Governor Stitt and the executive branch honor their oath.
questions.quote: Any federal statute, regulation, executive order or court decision which purports to supersede, stay or overrule this Act is in violation of the Constitution of the State of Oklahoma and the Constitution of the United States of America and is therefore void.

questions.question: Is Scott Klusendorf’s sex-slavery thought experiment a fair analogy for the abolitionist vs. incrementalist debate?
questions.audience: Christian wrestling with judicial supremacy and Romans 13
questions.answer: No. Klusendorf depicts incrementalists as saving 97% of victims and abolitionists as blocking that rescue, but in reality pro-life leaders are passing bills that save zero or maybe one percent and are actively killing the bills that would actually abolish abortion.
questions.quote: So you’ve got the pro-life leaders passing weak sauce bills which maybe reduce abortion by one percent and they call that winning. And then you’ve got abolitionists saying, “Maybe we should actually call abortion murder and treat it like murder with the bills we put forward.” Then the pro-life leaders kill those bills that would totally and immediately abolish abortion. That’s the real situation. **Klusendorf knows he can’t defend that so he makes up a thought experiment that bears no semblance to the reality.**

questions.question: Aren’t Heartbeat Bills and the Alabama Human Life Protection Act good incremental wins that save many babies?
questions.audience: Pro-life Oklahoman trying to evaluate SB13 honestly
questions.answer: Silberman argues these bills don’t save any babies because they fully submit to the Supreme Court, which has reaffirmed Roe-style protections 37 times, and Planned Parenthood proved the point by breaking ground on a new Birmingham mega facility right after Alabama’s ban.
questions.quote: The problem is that these bills actually don’t save *any babies*. All of these bills submit fully to the supreme court, and the federal courts have reaffirmed their disagreement with the idea that the states can ban abortion in the first or second trimester 37 times over. There’s little-to-no chance the courts are going to allow those bills to stand and everybody knows it.

questions.question: Do pro-life politicians actually lack the power to immediately end abortion, as Klusendorf and Joe Carter claim?
questions.audience: State legislator considering an abolition bill
questions.answer: No. The Constitution does not place the courts above the other branches, and politicians retain a constitutional and moral right and obligation to abolish abortion regardless of the Supreme Court’s opinion.
questions.quote: What Jefferson warned of is exactly what we have; a single tribunal of nine, unaccountable politically connected lawyers in black robes who ignore the Constitution and force all 50 states to allow the mass murder of preborn human beings. It’s despotic and it’s evil and we’re guilty of allowing it to go on for this long. Not only do we have a Constitutional and moral *right* to stand against it by immediately abolishing abortion, we have a Constitutional and moral *obligation*to stand against it by immediately abolishing abortion.

questions.question: How does the Bible speak to obeying a court that legalizes child sacrifice?
questions.audience: Christian wrestling with judicial supremacy and Romans 13
questions.answer: Scripture commands believers to defend the weak and to obey God rather than men when civil authorities order what God forbids; the pro-life establishment’s insistence on unconditional submission to Roe v. Wade reflects fear of man and idolatry of the courts.
questions.quote: To abolish abortion in Oklahoma, we must stop worrying about what the supreme court will do to us if we abolish the mass murder of preborn human beings and start worrying about what God will do to us if we don’t.

questions.question: If pro-life politicians dominate Oklahoma government, why hasn’t abortion been abolished there?
questions.audience: Pro-life Oklahoman trying to evaluate SB13 honestly
questions.answer: Because the pro-life establishment, not Planned Parenthood or NARAL, is what stands in the way. Republican supermajorities mean pro-life leaders have full power to abolish abortion and are simply choosing not to.
questions.quote: Despite what Klusendorf seems to wants you to  believe, the pro-life politicians and lobbyists in Oklahoma are the ones with the power to abolish abortion or to allow it. **They simply are choosing to allow it.**

questions.question: Does SB13 require Oklahoma’s government to ignore the Oklahoma Constitution?
questions.audience: State legislator considering an abolition bill
questions.answer: No. Both the Oklahoma and U.S. Constitutions protect the right to life; SB13 simply requires officials to take their constitutional oath seriously and refuse to obey court opinions that invent a right to murder.
questions.quote: The assertion that the pro-life politicians’ hands are tied by the Oklahoma Constitution is **pure fiction**, and ironically, they spread this fiction because they’re afraid to stand against the courts by standing firm on the what the US and Oklahoma Constitutions actually say – that the right to life and equal protection of the laws for all are guaranteed and unalienable.

questions.question: Is nullification really a legacy of slavery supporters as abolitiontruth.org claims?
questions.audience: Historian or civics-minded reader skeptical of nullification
questions.answer: The opposite is true. In the years before slavery’s abolition, nullification was practiced by abolitionists against the Fugitive Slave Act in states like Wisconsin, Vermont, Michigan, and Massachusetts, while slave owners and Confederates like Jefferson Davis bitterly opposed it.
questions.quote: In the years preceding slavery’s abolition, nullification was practiced exclusively by abolitionists to the acute dismay of the slave states. To oppose nullification in the context of American history is to side with the slavers.

questions.question: What concrete historical example shows abolitionists using nullification successfully?
questions.audience: Historian or civics-minded reader skeptical of nullification
questions.answer: The 1854 rescue of escaped slave Joshua Glover in Wisconsin, where 5,000 abolitionists rallied, broke Glover out of jail, sent him to freedom in Canada, and saw the Wisconsin Supreme Court declare the Fugitive Slave Act void, nullifying it in the state.
questions.quote: Paine, the author of the *habeas* *corpus* writ, defended Booth in court by arguing the Fugitive Slave Act was unconstitutional and therefore void. The State Supreme Court agreed and acquitted Booth. The Fugitive Slave Act had been nullified in Wisconsin.

questions.question: Is Senate President Greg Treat consistent in his attacks on nullification?
questions.audience: State legislator considering an abolition bill
questions.answer: No. Treat condemned nullification as the work of villains in committee but in 2016 himself authored a bill nullifying federal EPA regulations, exposing the hypocrisy of his historical claim.
questions.quote: There’s egregious hypocrisy here as Treat [authored a bill](https://blog.tenthamendmentcenter.com/2016/03/oklahoma-senate-passes-bill-to-block-implementation-of-epa-clean-power-plan-rules/) nullifying federal regulations in 2016, so he likely doesn’t even believe what he’s saying. But worse are the lies he’s spreading about American history which are exactly opposite of the truth. In the slavery debate, nullifiers were the heroes – their opponents the villains.

questions.question: Won’t there be massive pro-choice blowback in Oklahoma if abortion is immediately abolished?
questions.audience: Pro-life Oklahoman trying to evaluate SB13 honestly
questions.answer: Silberman argues Oklahoma is uniquely ready: the pro-abortion lobby has no power, pro-life bills pass nearly unanimously, and thousands of activists have publicly demanded immediate abolition at the State Capitol.
questions.quote: Oklahoma *is* ready for that change. A thousand people [demanded abortion’s immediate abolition at the State Capitol last year](https://www.facebook.com/FreeTheStates/videos/2284734625137050/) and thousands will be there on [February 11](https://www.facebook.com/events/490685208146928/) this year. Again, pro-life bills pass in Oklahoma nearly unanimously. The pro-abortion lobby has no power here. There will be no significant leftist blowback from abolishing abortion in the State of Oklahoma.

questions.question: Have abolitionists really accomplished nothing since Roe v. Wade, as Joe Carter claims?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activist needing rebuttals to common attacks
questions.answer: Carter’s charge is a sleight of hand because he hides who is actually blocking abolition bills: pro-life leaders themselves, who have killed abolition bills in Oklahoma, Idaho, Indiana, and Texas.
questions.quote: Carter conveniently fails to mention the reason why a bill of total and immediate abolition hasn’t passed: the pro-life leaders that he defends. As mentioned earlier, in Oklahoma, NARAL and Planned Parenthood have no political power. They cannot keep abortion from being abolished if the pro-life politicians decide to abolish abortion. But it’s the pro-life politicians and lobbyists who are fighting against abortion’s abolition *for* the abortion industry.

questions.question: Why does Silberman insist these are deceptions rather than honest mistakes?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activist needing rebuttals to common attacks
questions.answer: Because the people behind abolitiontruth.org are professional politicians and lobbyists who refuse to identify themselves, and they make claims (like denying SB13’s inseverability clause) that they demonstrably know are false.
questions.quote: A recently published website, abolitiontruth.org, exemplifies the blatant corruption of many of the leaders of the pro-life movement. The website’s publishers do not identify themselves, likely so that they don’t have to answer for their dishonesty. Abolitiontruth.org contains eight huge deceptions about SB13 and about the Abolitionist Movement.

questions.question: What does Silberman say is the single condition under which abortion will actually be abolished?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activist needing rebuttals to common attacks
questions.answer: The day pro-life politicians and lobbyists, including those running abolitiontruth.org, stop opposing total and immediate abolition is the day abortion will be abolished.
questions.quote: The day pro-life politicians and lobbyists, including those who made abolitiontruth.org, stop opposing the total and immediate abolition of abortion is the day abortion will be abolished. Pray that day comes soon.

related.title: Why "No Bill But SB13"?
related.reason: Lays out the positive case for SB13 that this article defends against the abolitiontruth.org deceptions.

related.title: Are Nullification’s Advocates the “Villains” of American History?
related.reason: Expands the historical argument used in Deception #6 that abolitionists, not slavers, were the nullifiers.

related.title: Pastor and Gubernatorial Candidate Dan Fisher Responds to Oklahoma Baptist Leaders Opposing SB13
related.reason: Directly addresses the Oklahoma Baptist leaders Silberman names as spreading the inseverability lie.

related.title: Five Reasons Abolitionists Oppose Incremental Pro-Life Bills
related.reason: Develops the abolitionist critique of incrementalism Silberman levels at Klusendorf in Deception #3.

related.title: Contrary to Treat's Media Tour, SB13 is Not a Secession Bill
related.reason: Continues the rebuttal of Senate President Greg Treat’s misinformation about SB13 and nullification.

related.title: Pro-Life Idaho State Rep: "We all know nullification doesn't happen in reality."
related.reason: Documents the same anti-nullification talking point used by the pro-life establishment outside Oklahoma.

related.title: Nullification
related.reason: A topical reference page on nullification, the constitutional doctrine at the heart of SB13 and Deception #6.
